questions.prompt: Three dimensions of conversion signal quality?

choices.text: Coverage (tracking the events that actually matter, including upstream predictors), accuracy (events firing once with correct values, GCLID present, deduplicated), and differentiation (different conversion types and values distinguished — a $50 lead and a $5,000 lead are not the same). Smart Bidding can only optimize toward what you measure; thin/inaccurate/undifferentiated signal caps performance regardless of strategy choice.

questions.explanation: Coverage, accuracy, differentiation. ML can only optimize what you measure.

questions.prompt: E-commerce account has Purchase, Add to Cart, View Product all marked as 'Include in Conversions' (primary). CPA fine but new-customer revenue weak. Cause?

choices.text: Optimization is diluted. Including Add to Cart and View Product as primary means Smart Bidding bids toward those events alongside Purchase — the system finds users who cart and view, not buyers. Reported 'conversions' look high but new-customer revenue lags because most counted conversions never paid. Fix: set Purchase as the only primary (Include); set Add to Cart and View Product to 'See in conversions reporting' / secondary. Smart Bidding now optimizes solely for sales; secondary events still feed audiences and reporting.

questions.explanation: Too many primary events dilute the optimization. Pick the real outcome.
